ElementDescription: What suggestions do you have for how to make this program better?
Interpersonal Psychotherapy-Adolescent Skills Training (IPT-AST) is based on the premise that:
Notes: 1=Depression results primarily from maladaptive cognitive patterns; 2=Depression results primarily from chemical imbalances requiring medication; 3=Depression can impact people's relationships and people's relationships can affect their mood; 4=Depression is maintained by skill deficits related to emotional regulation; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: The primary goal of IPT-AST is to prevent depression and promote emotional well-being by teaching adolescents which set of skills?
Notes: 1=Relaxation and mindfulness strategies to cope with stress; 2=Communication and interpersonal problem-solving skills to improve relationships; 3=Cognitive restructuring techniques to challenge negative thoughts; 4=Time management and organizational skills to reduce academic stress; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: When completing the closeness circle, what should the group leader do?
Notes: 1=The leader should ask detailed questions about interactions and expectations for each relationship; 2=The leader should only ask a brief question or two about identifying information (e.g., name and age); 3=The leader should explain the core framework of IPT-AST (mood affects relationships and relationships affect mood) for each person entered in the circle; 4=The leader should ask if the teen would like to set a goal for each person written in the circle; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: When conducting the interpersonal inventory, it is recommended that the group leader:
Notes: 1=Discuss important relationships from the teen's past; 2=Discuss only those relationships in the inner circle of the closeness circle; 3=Discuss every individual that the teen places on the closeness circle in great detail; 4=Discuss relationships that are important to the teen or those that impact their mood, ideally covering a variety of types of relationships (e.g., family, friends); -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: The process of goal-setting is described as being ''collaborative'' between the leader and the adolescent. What primary purpose does collaboration serve?
Notes: 1=It ensures the leader has full control over what the teen choses as goals; 2= It helps the adolescent take ownership of the goals, while the leader suggests goals that may be most useful based on the interpersonal inventory; 3=It allows the adolescent to set goals that are entirely separate from the findings of the interpersonal inventory; 4=It guarantees that the teen will commit to talking to their parents about their feelings; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: How do you feel about the length of the total program (8 group sessions plus 3 individual sessions)?
Notes: 1=The length of the program was much too short; 2=The length of the program was a little too short; 3=The length of the program was just right; 4=The length of the program was a little too long; 5=The length of the program was much too long; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: What are the primary goals of the initial phase of IPT-AST?
Notes: 1=Teaching youth to monitor their thoughts; 2=Sharing goals for group and mood ratings with other group members; 3=Doing targeted work on interpersonal goals; 4=Getting to know other group members and learning communication strategies; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: During Group Session 2, adolescents role-play hypothetical interpersonal situations. What specific aspect of communication does this activity primarily aim to illustrate?
Notes: 1=That brainstorming multiple solutions is the most effective way to resolve conflict and improve mood; 2=That openly sharing deeply personal feelings early in the group builds rapport and makes communication easier; 3=That both verbal and nonverbal communication influence how others respond to us and the outcomes of our conversations; 4=That considering the other person's thoughts and feelings is the most effective way to resolve conflict and improve mood; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: The technique of communication analysis, as introduced in Session 2, involves:
Notes: 1=Focusing only on nonverbal cues during an interaction; 2=Providing immediate solutions to communication problems; 3=Breaking down the play by play of a conversation; 4=Planning for a follow-up conversation; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: Which of the following is the most effective example of an ''I statement'' as taught in IPT-AST?
Notes: 1=You always make me angry when you interrupt me; 2=I feel angry when you interrupt me; 3=I feel like you are being rude when you talk over me; 4=I am just so annoyed; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: The strategy ''Put yourself in their shoes'' is described as a negotiation tool that involves two steps. What are these two steps?
Notes: 1=Expressing your feelings and requesting a compromise; 2=Identifying the other person's perspective and explaining why they are wrong; 3=Thinking about how the other person feels and acknowledging that out loud; 4=Acknowledging how the other person feels and offering a solution to the problem; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: Besides the general mood rating, what else are teens asked to share during the mood check-in?
Notes: 1=Their specific goal for the session; 2=Whether they want to participate in a role-play during the session; 3=The results of any conversations they had on their own in the past week; 4=An interpersonal event in the past week that has affected their mood in a positive or negative way; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: What is involved in setting the agenda in a middle phase session?
Notes: 1=Conduct a communication analysis; 2=Role play the conversation; 3=Identify an interpersonal situation to work on; 4=Use decision analysis; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: When scripting a conversation, what is generally considered the most helpful approach for the teen?
Notes: 1=Planning to redo a past conversation that didn't go well; 2=Planning a conversation that can happen proactively to improve the situation; 3=Scripting only the opening exchange of a planned conversation; 4=Planning a conversation in which it is assumed that the other person will respond favorably; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: What is the designated role of the ''coach'' during the role play?
Notes: 1=To help the teen remember which planned communication strategies to use by holding up cue cards; 2=To debrief the conversation and offer suggestions for improvement afterward; 3=To play the part of the other person if the role-player struggles to respond accurately; 4=To take notes on the exchanges that did not go well for the leader to analyze later; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: When should Interpersonal Work at Home be assigned to group members?
Notes: 1=It is assigned only if the role-play was unsuccessful; 2=It is assigned to group members who worked on an interpersonal situation during the session; 3=It is only assigned after the individual mid-group sessions are complete; 4=It is assigned to all group members at the end of every middle phase session; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: How relevant were the topics discussed in Rising Together to your students?

ElementDescription: What are the primary goals of the individual mid-group session?
Notes: 1=To review and work on the teen's goals; 2=To help the teen identify group members they could be friends with after group; 3=To assess for symptoms of depression in a more private setting to provide more personalized referrals for outside care; 4=To meet the teen's parents in order to improve the accuracy of role plays conducted in group; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: Following a successful role-play during an individual mid-group session, what is the goal of the work-at-home assignment given to the teen?
Notes: 1=To write down a list of new interpersonal goals; 2=To teach the communication strategies to someone else in their life; 3=To perform a communication analysis on a past argument; 4=To have the conversation the teen role-played during the session so the teen can report back to the group on the outcome; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: Which of the following is an essential component of the Termination Phase of IPT-AST?
Notes: 1=Having each group member rate the progress made toward their goals on a scale of 1-10; 2=Giving each group member a written progress report of their accomplishments and continued areas of growth; 3=Referring all group members to additional services; 4=Discussing the interpersonal strategies that were most useful and most challenging; -9=Prefer not to answer

ElementDescription: During the Termination Phase of IPT-AST, what is the primary purpose of having the group members engage in the activity of writing down a positive characteristic for each participant (including the leader)?
Notes: 1=To create a final written record of everyone in the group; 2=To provide the leader with feedback on their effectiveness as a group facilitator; 3=To help teens identify positive characteristics to look for in relationships; 4=To practice using I statements in a supportive environment; -9=Prefer not to answer
